003 * contributor license agreements.  See the NOTICE file distributed with
004 * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.

006 * (the "License"); you may not use this file except in compliance with
007 * the License.  You may obtain a copy of the License at
008 *
009 *      http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
010 *
011 * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
012 * distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
013 * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
014 * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
015 * limitations under the License.
016 */
017package org.apache.juneau.marshall.markdown;
018
019import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.AssertionUtils.*;
020
021import java.io.*;
022import java.lang.reflect.*;
023import java.time.*;
024import java.time.temporal.*;
025import java.util.*;
026
027import org.apache.juneau.commons.bean.*;
028import org.apache.juneau.marshall.*;
029import org.apache.juneau.marshall.json.*;
030import org.apache.juneau.marshall.json5.*;
031import org.apache.juneau.marshall.serializer.*;
032import org.apache.juneau.marshall.stream.*;
033import org.apache.juneau.marshall.swap.*;
034
035/**
036 * Session object for {@link MarkdownSerializer}.
037 *
038 * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
039 *    <li class='warn'>This class is not thread safe and is typically discarded after one use.
040 * </ul>
041 *
042 */
043@SuppressWarnings({
044   "java:S110",  // Inheritance depth acceptable for serializer session hierarchy
045   "java:S115",  // Constants use UPPER_snakeCase convention
046   "java:S3740", // Raw ClassMeta/ObjectSwap types used throughout serializer session; parameterization requires extensive generic changes
047   "java:S3776", // Cognitive complexity acceptable for doSerialize
048   "java:S6541", // Brain method acceptable for doSerialize / serializeAnything
049   "resource",   // MarkdownWriter/Writer lifecycle managed by SerializerPipe
050   "rawtypes",
051})
052public class MarkdownSerializerSession extends WriterSerializerSession implements RecordWritable {
053
054   private static final String ARG_ctx = "ctx";
055
056   final String nullValue;
057   final boolean showHeaders;
058
059   /**
060    * Builder class.
061    */
062   @SuppressWarnings({
063      "java:S110", // Inheritance depth acceptable for serializer session builder hierarchy
064      "java:S119" // 'SELF' (CRTP self-type) is intentional and clearer than a single-letter name.
065   })
066   public abstract static class Builder<SELF extends Builder<SELF>> extends WriterSerializerSession.Builder<SELF> {
067
068      String nullValue;
069      boolean showHeaders;
070
071      /**
072       * Constructor.
073       *
074       * @param ctx The context creating this session.
075       *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
076       */
077      protected Builder(MarkdownSerializer ctx) {
078         super(assertArgNotNull(ARG_ctx, ctx));
079         nullValue = ctx.getNullValue();
080         showHeaders = ctx.isShowHeaders();
081      }
082
083      @Override
084      public MarkdownSerializerSession build() {
085         return new MarkdownSerializerSession(this);
086      }
087
088   }
089
090   /**
091    * Concrete default builder leaf for the non-subclassed {@code create()} path (CRTP terminal).
092    */
093   public static final class DefaultBuilder extends Builder<DefaultBuilder> {
094
095      DefaultBuilder(MarkdownSerializer ctx) {
096         super(ctx);
097      }
098   }
099
100   /**
101    * Creates a new builder for this object.
102    *
103    * @param ctx The context creating this session.
104    *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
105    * @return A new builder.
106    */
107   @SuppressWarnings({
108      "java:S1452" // Builder<?> wildcard return intentional; callers use it to construct session instances polymorphically
109   })
110   public static Builder<?> create(MarkdownSerializer ctx) {
111      return new DefaultBuilder(assertArgNotNull(ARG_ctx, ctx));
112   }
113
114   /**
115    * Constructor.
116    *
117    * @param builder The builder for this object.
118    */
119   protected MarkdownSerializerSession(Builder<?> builder) {
120      super(builder);
121      nullValue = builder.nullValue != null ? builder.nullValue : "*null*";
122      showHeaders = builder.showHeaders;
123   }
124
125   @Override /* RecordWritable */
126   public RecordWriter serializeRecords(Object output) throws IOException {
127      return RecordAdapter.writer(this, output);
128   }
129
130   @Override /* RecordWritable */
131   public boolean isRecordStreaming() {
132      return false;
133   }
134
135   @Override /* Overridden from SerializerSession */
136   protected void doSerialize(SerializerPipe pipe, Object o) throws IOException, SerializeException {
137      try (var w = getMarkdownWriter(pipe)) {
138         serializeAnything(w, o, getExpectedRootType(o), null);
139      }
140   }
141
142   /**
143    * Main serialization dispatch method.
144    *
145    * @param w The writer to write to.
146    * @param o The object to serialize.
147    * @param eType The expected type of the object.
148    * @param pMeta The bean property meta (may be null).
149    * @throws IOException Thrown by underlying stream.
150    * @throws SerializeException If serialization fails.
151    */
152   protected void serializeAnything(MarkdownWriter w, Object o, ClassMeta<?> eType, BeanPropertyMeta pMeta) throws IOException, SerializeException {
153      if (o == null) {
154         w.text(nullValue);
155         return;
156      }
157
158      ClassMeta cm = push2("root", o, eType);
159      if (cm == null) {
160         // Recursion detected
161         pop();
162         w.text(nullValue);
163         return;
164      }
165
166      ObjectSwap swap = cm.getSwap(this);
167      if (swap != null) {
168         o = swap(swap, o);
169         cm = swap.getSwapClassMeta(this);
170      }
171
172      if (cm.isOptional()) {
173         var opt = (Optional<?>) o;
174         if (opt.isEmpty()) {
175            pop();
176            w.text(nullValue);
177         } else {
178            serializeAnything(w, opt.get(), eType, pMeta);
179            pop();
180         }
181         return;
182      }
183
184      if (cm.isBean()) {
185         serializeBeanMap(w, toBeanMap(o), eType, cm);
186      } else if (cm.isMap()) {
187         serializeMap(w, (Map<?,?>) o, cm);
188      } else if (cm.isCollectionOrArray()) {
189         var l = cm.isArray() ? toObjectList(o) : (Collection<?>) o;
190         serializeCollection(w, l, eType, cm);
191      } else if (cm.isStreamable()) {
192         serializeCollection(w, toListFromStreamable(o, cm), eType, cm);
193      } else if (cm.isDate()) {
194         w.text(serializeDate((Date)o, cm));
195      } else if (cm.isCalendar()) {
196         w.text(serializeCalendar(o, cm));
197      } else if (cm.isTemporal()) {
198         w.text(serializeTemporal((TemporalAccessor)o, cm));
199      } else if (cm.isDuration()) {
200         w.text(serializeDuration((Duration)o));
201      } else if (cm.isPeriod()) {
202         w.text(serializePeriod((Period)o));
203      } else {
204         var s = toString(o);
205         // Only use JSON5 wrapping for actual string/enum types where auto-detection could misinterpret
206         // the value; non-bean objects (e.g. those with a valueOf(String) factory) use plain text so
207         // that the parser can use convertToType() on the raw string.
208         if ((cm.isCharSequence() || cm.isEnum()) && isAmbiguousString(s, nullValue))
209            w.text("`'" + escapeJson5String(s) + "'`");
210         else
211            w.text(MarkdownWriter.escapeText(s));
212      }
213      
214      pop();
215   }
216
217   /**
218    * Serializes a bean as a 2-column key/value Markdown table.
219    *
220    * @param w The writer.
221    * @param bm The bean map.
222    * @param eType The expected type (for type name calculation).
223    * @param aType The actual type (for type name calculation).
224    * @throws IOException Thrown by underlying stream.
225    * @throws SerializeException If serialization fails.
226    */
227   protected void serializeBeanMap(MarkdownWriter w, BeanMap<?> bm, ClassMeta<?> eType, ClassMeta<?> aType) throws IOException, SerializeException {
228      if (showHeaders) {
229         w.tableHeader("Property", "Value");
230         w.tableSeparator(2);
231      }
232      
233      // Add _type row if needed for polymorphic type identification
234      // This handles both addBeanTypes (eType != aType) and addRootType (root object with dict name)
235      String typeName = getBeanTypeName(this, eType, aType, null);
236      if (typeName != null) {
237         w.tableRow("_type", typeName);
238      }
239      
240      for (var entry : bm.entrySet()) {
241         if (entry instanceof BeanMapEntry bme && !bme.getMeta().canRead())
242            continue;
243         w.tableRow(MarkdownWriter.escapeCell(entry.getKey()), serializeInlineValue(entry.getValue()));
244      }
245   }
246
247   /**
248    * Serializes a Map as a 2-column key/value Markdown table.
249    *
250    * @param w The writer.
251    * @param m The map.
252    * @param cm The class meta.
253    * @throws IOException Thrown by underlying stream.
254    * @throws SerializeException If serialization fails.
255    */
256   protected void serializeMap(MarkdownWriter w, Map<?,?> m, ClassMeta<?> cm) throws IOException, SerializeException {
257      if (showHeaders) {
258         w.tableHeader("Key", "Value");
259         w.tableSeparator(2);
260      }
261      for (var entry : m.entrySet()) {
262         String rawKey = entry.getKey() == null ? null : toString(entry.getKey());
263         String keyStr;
264         if (rawKey == null)
265            keyStr = nullValue;
266         else if (isAmbiguousString(rawKey, nullValue))
267            keyStr = "`'" + escapeJson5String(rawKey) + "'`";
268         else
269            keyStr = MarkdownWriter.escapeCell(rawKey);
270         w.tableRow(keyStr, serializeInlineValue(entry.getValue()));
271      }
272   }
273
274   /**
275    * Serializes a collection as either a multi-column table (uniform beans/maps) or a bulleted list.
276    *
277    * @param w The writer.
278    * @param c The collection.
279    * @param eType The expected type.
280    * @param cm The class meta.
281    * @throws IOException Thrown by underlying stream.
282    * @throws SerializeException If serialization fails.
283    */
284   protected void serializeCollection(MarkdownWriter w, Collection<?> c, ClassMeta<?> eType, ClassMeta<?> cm) throws IOException, SerializeException {
285      if (c.isEmpty())
286         return;
287
288      var headers = getTableHeaders(c);
289      if (headers != null) {
290         // Uniform collection of beans or maps → multi-column table
291         
292         // Determine if we need a _type column
293         boolean needsTypeColumn = isAddBeanTypes();
294         var elementType = eType != null && eType.isCollectionOrArray() ? eType.getElementType() : cm.getElementType();
295
296         // Add _type column to headers if needed
297         String[] finalHeaders = headers;
298         if (needsTypeColumn) {
299            finalHeaders = new String[headers.length + 1];
300            finalHeaders[0] = "_type";
301            System.arraycopy(headers, 0, finalHeaders, 1, headers.length);
302         }
303
304         if (showHeaders && finalHeaders.length > 0) {
305            w.tableHeader(finalHeaders);
306            w.tableSeparator(finalHeaders.length);
307         }
308
309         for (var item : c) {
310            if (item == null) {
311               var row = new String[finalHeaders.length];
312               Arrays.fill(row, nullValue);
313               w.tableRow(row);
314               continue;
315            }
316            ClassMeta itemCm = getClassMetaForObject(item);
317            ObjectSwap itemSwap = itemCm.getSwap(this);
318            var swapped = itemSwap != null ? swap(itemSwap, item) : item;
319            ClassMeta swappedCm = itemSwap != null ? itemSwap.getSwapClassMeta(this) : itemCm;
320
321            var row = new String[finalHeaders.length];
322            int colOffset = needsTypeColumn ? 1 : 0;
323
324            // Add type name if needed
325            // For root-level collections with addRootType, treat each element as root
326            if (needsTypeColumn) {
327               String typeName;
328               if (isRoot() && isAddRootType()) {
329                  // Root-level collection: add type name if the bean has a dictionary name
330                  typeName = swappedCm.getBeanDictionaryName();
331               } else {
332                  // Nested collection: use standard logic
333                  typeName = getBeanTypeName(this, elementType, swappedCm, null);
334               }
335               row[0] = typeName != null ? typeName : "";
336            }
337
338            if (swappedCm.isBean()) {
339               var bm = toBeanMap(swapped);
340               for (var i = 0; i < headers.length; i++) {
341                  var val = bm.get(headers[i]);
342                  row[i + colOffset] = serializeInlineValue(val);
343               }
344            } else if (swappedCm.isMap()) {
345               var map = (Map<?,?>) swapped;
346               for (var i = 0; i < headers.length; i++) {
347                  var val = map.get(headers[i]);
348                  row[i + colOffset] = serializeInlineValue(val);
349               }
350            }
351            w.tableRow(row);
352         }
353      } else {
354         // Mixed or simple values → bulleted list
355         for (var item : c)
356            w.bulletItem(0, serializeInlineValue(item));
357      }
358   }
359
360   /**
361    * Returns true if the string could be misinterpreted during parsing when the target type is Object,
362    * or if the string contains characters that would be lost or corrupted in Markdown table cells.
363    *
364    * <p>Strings that are empty, equal to nullValue, look like booleans or numbers, or contain control
365    * characters (< 32 or = 127) are considered ambiguous and should be wrapped in JSON5 backtick syntax.
366    */
367   private static boolean isAmbiguousString(String s, String nullValue) {
368      if (s.isEmpty()) return true;
369      if (s.equals(nullValue)) return true;
370      if ("true".equals(s) || "false".equals(s)) return true;
371      for (int i = 0; i < s.length(); i++) {
372         char c = s.charAt(i);
373         if (c < 32 || c == 127) return true;
374      }
375      try { Integer.parseInt(s); return true; } catch (@SuppressWarnings("unused") NumberFormatException ignored) { /* Not a number; fall through */ }
376      try { Long.parseLong(s); return true; } catch (@SuppressWarnings("unused") NumberFormatException ignored) { /* Not a number; fall through */ }
377      try { Double.parseDouble(s); return true; } catch (@SuppressWarnings("unused") NumberFormatException ignored) { /* Not a number; fall through */ }
378      return false;
379   }
380
381   /**
382    * Escapes a string for use within a JSON5 single-quoted string literal.
383    */
384   private static String escapeJson5String(String s) {
385      if (s == null) return "";
386      var sb = new StringBuilder(s.length() + 4);
387      for (var i = 0; i < s.length(); i++) {
388         var c = s.charAt(i);
389         switch (c) {
390            case '\'' -> sb.append("\\'");
391            case '\\' -> sb.append("\\\\");
392            case '\n' -> sb.append("\\n");
393            case '\r' -> sb.append("\\r");
394            case '\t' -> sb.append("\\t");
395            default -> sb.append(c);
396         }
397      }
398      return sb.toString();
399   }
400
401   /**
402    * Converts an array (including primitive arrays) to a List of boxed objects.
403    */
404   private static List<Object> toObjectList(Object array) {
405      int len = Array.getLength(array);
406      var list = new ArrayList<>(len);
407      for (int i = 0; i < len; i++)
408         list.add(Array.get(array, i));
409      return list;
410   }
411
412   /**
413    * Returns a Json5Serializer that shares this session's bean context (swaps, implClasses, dictionary).
414    * Creates a new serializer each time to ensure settings are current.
415    */
416   private JsonSerializer getJson5Serializer() {
417      var b = Json5Serializer.create().marshallingContext((MarshallingContext) getContext());
418      if (isAddBeanTypes())
419         b = b.addBeanTypes();
420      if (isAddRootType())
421         b = b.addRootType();
422      if (isTrimStrings())
423         b = b.trimStrings();
424      return b.build();
425   }
426
427   /**
428    * Serializes a value for placement in a Markdown table cell.
429    *
430    * <p>
431    * Simple values (strings, numbers, booleans, enums, dates) are escaped and returned inline.
432    * Complex values (beans, maps, collections) are serialized as JSON5 in backticks.
433    *
434    * @param o The value to serialize.
435    * @return The cell string value.
436    * @throws SerializeException If serialization fails.
437    */
438   protected String serializeInlineValue(Object o) throws SerializeException {
439      if (o == null)
440         return nullValue;
441
442      ClassMeta cm = getClassMetaForObject(o);
443      ObjectSwap swap = cm.getSwap(this);
444      if (swap != null) {
445         o = swap(swap, o);
446         cm = swap.getSwapClassMeta(this);
447      }
448
449      if (cm.isCharSequence() || cm.isEnum()) {
450         var s = toString(o);
451         if (isAmbiguousString(s, nullValue))
452            return "`'" + escapeJson5String(s) + "'`";
453         return MarkdownWriter.escapeCell(s);
454      }
455
456      if (cm.isNumber() || cm.isBoolean())
457         return o.toString();
458
459      if (cm.isDate())
460         return MarkdownWriter.escapeCell(serializeDate((Date)o, cm));
461
462      if (cm.isCalendar())
463         return MarkdownWriter.escapeCell(serializeCalendar(o, cm));
464
465      if (cm.isTemporal())
466         return MarkdownWriter.escapeCell(serializeTemporal((TemporalAccessor)o, cm));
467
468      if (cm.isDuration())
469         return MarkdownWriter.escapeCell(serializeDuration((Duration)o));
470
471      if (cm.isPeriod())
472         return MarkdownWriter.escapeCell(serializePeriod((Period)o));
473
474      // Complex value: serialize as JSON5 in backticks — use context-aware serializer for swaps
475      try {
476         var json5 = getJson5Serializer().serialize(o);
477         return "`" + json5 + "`";
478      } catch (@SuppressWarnings("unused") SerializeException e) {
479         return MarkdownWriter.escapeCell(o.toString());
480      }
481   }
482
483   /**
484    * Determines if a collection can be rendered as a multi-column table.
485    *
486    * <p>
487    * Returns the column header names if the collection is a uniform set of beans or maps,
488    * or {@code null} if it should be rendered as a bulleted list.
489    *
490    * @param c The collection to inspect.
491    * @return The column header names, or {@code null} for list rendering.
492    * @throws SerializeException If inspection fails.
493    */
494   @SuppressWarnings({
495      "java:S1168" // null signals list rendering; empty array would incorrectly trigger table mode
496   })
497   protected String[] getTableHeaders(Collection<?> c) throws SerializeException {
498      if (c.isEmpty())
499         return null;
500
501      // Find first non-null element
502      Object first = null;
503      for (var item : c) {
504         if (item != null) {
505            first = item;
506            break;
507         }
508      }
509      if (first == null)
510         return null;
511
512      ClassMeta cm = getClassMetaForObject(first);
513      ObjectSwap swap = cm.getSwap(this);
514      if (swap != null) {
515         try {
516            first = swap(swap, first);
517            cm = swap.getSwapClassMeta(this);
518         } catch (@SuppressWarnings("unused") SerializeException e) {
519            return null;
520         }
521      }
522
523      if (!cm.isMapOrBean())
524         return null;
525
526      if (cm.isBean()) {
527         var bm = toBeanMap(first);
528         return bm.keySet().toArray(new String[0]);
529      }
530
531      // Non-bean map: collect keys from first entry
532      if (first instanceof Map<?,?> map2) {
533         return map2.keySet().stream().map(Object::toString).toArray(String[]::new);
534      }
535
536      return null;
537   }
538
539   /**
540    * Gets or creates a {@link MarkdownWriter} from the given pipe.
541    *
542    * @param out The serializer pipe.
543    * @return The writer.
544    */
545   protected MarkdownWriter getMarkdownWriter(SerializerPipe out) {
546      var output = out.getRawOutput();
547      if (output instanceof MarkdownWriter output2)
548         return output2;
549      var w = new MarkdownWriter(out.getWriter(), isUseWhitespace(), getMaxIndent(), isTrimStrings(), getUriResolver());
550      out.setWriter(w);
551      return w;
552   }
553}
